days.title: Arrival in Paris
days.subtitle: Exploring the City of Lights

timeline.title: Arrive at Gare du Nord
timeline.detail: Check into Paris at Gare du Nord, one of the busiest railway stations in Europe and a hub for Eurostar services. Follow the signs to the Metro to transfer to your hotel.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Le Petit Paris
timeline.detail: Settle in at this charming hotel located just a 10-minute walk from the Montparnasse station. Enjoy cozy modern rooms and a warm Parisian ambiance.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Le Petit Paris, Paris, France

timeline.title: Visit the Eiffel Tower
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely walk to the iconic Eiffel Tower. Enjoy a view of this architectural marvel from Trocadéro Gardens, and consider pre-booking your ticket for a lift to the summit.
timeline.mapQuery: Eiffel Tower, Champ de Mars, Paris

timeline.title: Dinner at Le Relais de l'Entrecôte
timeline.detail: Savor the famous steak-frites served with a secret sauce at this bustling brasserie. The ambiance is lively, so arrive early to avoid long waits. Reservation recommended.
timeline.mapQuery: Le Relais de l'Entrecôte, Paris, France

timeline.title: Seine River Night Cruise
timeline.detail: Embark on a magical nighttime cruise along the Seine River. Experience Paris's landmarks illuminated at night, including Notre Dame and the Louvre. Book tickets in advance for prime seating.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: After a delightful evening, stroll back to your hotel to rest up for the next busy day in Paris.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Le Petit Paris, Paris, France

localTips: Buy a carnet of 10 Metro tickets for ease of travel around the city.
Book Eiffel Tower tickets online to skip the long queues.
Try to learn a few French phrases; locals appreciate the effort.
Opt for restaurants away from main tourist spots for authentic experiences.
Check if your hotel offers city walking tours - they're often free!

days.title: Gastronomic Explorations
days.subtitle: Indulging in Parisian Cuisine

timeline.title: Breakfast at Café de Flore
timeline.detail: Start your day with a classic French breakfast at one of Paris's most historic cafés. Enjoy croissants, coffee, and people-watching in the vibrant Saint-Germain-des-Prés area.

timeline.title: Culinary Workshop at Le Foodist
timeline.detail: Attend a cooking class where you will learn to make traditional French dishes. This hands-on experience includes a market visit to select fresh ingredients.

timeline.title: Lunch at L’Atelier de Joël Robuchon
timeline.detail: Enjoy a Michelin-starred experience at this renowned restaurant. Taste the expertly prepared dishes that highlight the best ingredients and culinary techniques.
timeline.mapQuery: L’Atelier de Joël Robuchon, Paris, France

timeline.title: Explore the Latin Quarter
timeline.detail: After lunch, take a stroll through the historic Latin Quarter. Discover its narrow streets, bookstores, and the stunning Panthéon.

timeline.title: Dinner at Chez Janou
timeline.detail: Conclude your culinary journey with a delightful dinner at this charming bistro in Le Marais. Try their famous bouillabaisse and finish with a dessert from the pastry chef.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Depart for Gare du Nord
timeline.detail: After dinner, return to your hotel to collect your bags and head to Gare du Nord for your onward travel.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Le Petit Paris, Paris, France

localTips: Consider booking a guided food tour for an insider's culinary journey.
Use an offline map app for easier navigation around the city.
Tipping is appreciated but not obligatory; round up or leave small change.
Explore local markets like Rue Cler for a genuine Parisian experience.
Reserve restaurant tables in advance, especially for popular spots.

hotels.name: Hotel Le Petit Paris
hotels.note: Charming hotel near Montparnasse, cozy atmosphere.
hotels.address: 214 Rue de la Croix Nivert, 75015 Paris, France
